primary_focus: Problem validation and product-market fit search. Heavy strategic discovery with maximum evidence requirements. Multiple prototype variants to explore solution space before committing.

discovery_depth.rationale: Early-stage products need exhaustive discovery to avoid building the wrong thing. PESTLE validates market viability. Deep personas and journeys surface real user needs. Hypothesis portfolio manages uncertainty systematically.

strategy_depth.rationale: Strategy must produce multiple distinct options and subject each to premortem analysis. Single-frame strategy is anti-pattern at this stage.

planning_depth.rationale: Prototypes reduce the biggest source of risk: building the wrong thing. 3 variants cover the solution space. PRD stays focused on core value hypothesis.

execution_depth.rationale: Execution stays lean. Build only what validates the core hypothesis. No heavy process until PMF signal is strong.

launch_depth.rationale: Launch is about learning, not scale. Readiness check ensures minimum bar. Marketing content is lightweight.

support_depth.rationale: Outcome review is critical: did the hypothesis validate? Issue log captures real user friction for next iteration.

prototype_expectation.primary_purpose: Explore solution space breadth before committing to a single design direction. PM selects best-fit variant after side-by-side evaluation.

pm_review_thresholds: Any moderate or low confidence output
review_sensitivity.rationale: 0→1 is maximum review: product is fragile. Every decision is high-stakes. PM gate is active on nearly everything.

anti_patterns: Building without customer evidence — discovery must precede any build decision
Single-strategy-option thinking — must generate and evaluate multiple paths
Skipping prototype validation — unvalidated concepts committing to PRD
Over-polishing before PMF signal — v1 should be good, not perfect
